articles.title: West Bengal Removes 77 Muslim Communities From OBC List As Assembly Passes Two Key Amendment Bills
articles.description: The West Bengal Assembly has enacted two amendment bills that eliminate 77 Muslim communities from the OBC list, aligning with a Calcutta High Court ruling.

articles.description: Omar said that he has urged the intervention of his Punjab counterpart, CM Bhagwant Singh Mann, for early resolution to prevent unauthorised levies and protect the interests of traders and consumers in J&K

articles.title: 'Love To Have Him Back': Sergio Gor Confirms PM Modi's US Invitation, Hints At Trump India Visit Next Year
articles.description: US Ambassador Sergio Gor confirmed that PM Modi has been invited to visit the US again. He also hinted that Donald Trump could visit India next year.

articles.title: Voter verification drive begins in Delhi today; no documents to be submitted at this stage
articles.description: Delhi is undertaking a month-long special intensive revision of its electoral roll, with over 13,000 booth-level officers verifying nearly 1.5 crore electors door-to-door. The aim is to eliminate errors like duplicate entries. Special attention will be given to voters affected by recent demolitions. The draft roll will be published on August 5, with the final version expected by October 7.

articles.title: US SC says Fed's Cook can keep her job for now
articles.description: The Supreme Court has significantly broadened presidential authority, allowing presidents to dismiss heads of independent federal agencies at will, overturning a long-standing precedent. However, the Federal Reserve remains an exception, with a governor allowed to remain in her post while contesting her dismissal. This ruling grants presidents greater control over regulatory bodies, sparking concerns about political influence and potential instability.

articles.title: 'Gives people more time to vote illegally...': Trump hits out at mail-in ballot grace period ruling
articles.description: US President Donald Trump criticized a Supreme Court decision on Monday (June 29) that allows mail-in ballots received after Election Day to be counted, calling it “surprising” and “very detrimental to honest elections”. The court on Monday rejected a Republican-led challenge to a five-day grace period in Mississippi for election day ballots, dealing a setback to Trump. The justices in a 5-4 ruling overturned a lower court's decision that had deemed Mississippi's law inconsistent with U.S. statutes that set the timing of federal elections — for the presidency, Senate and House of Representatives.

articles.title: Vimal Negi death case: Wife seeks governor’s intervention, demands action against senior officials
articles.description: In a memorandum submitted to the governor, she demanded action against the then HPPCL managing director Harikesh Meena and director Deshraj regarding corruption allegations, an impartial investigation into the case along with action against those who allegedly tampered with evidence

articles.title: 'America Weak, Iran Stronger': U.S. Congman's Big Admission; Democrat Jeffries Lashes Out At Trump
articles.description: House Democratic leader Hakeem Jeffries accuses President Trump of dragging America into a "reckless and costly war of choice" with Iran. Jeffries argued the conflict has weakened U.S. national security, strengthened Iran's position, and driven up gas prices for American families. He called for an immediate classified briefing for every member of Congress on Iran and the broader Middle East situation. Jeffries also reaffirmed support for Israel's security while backing a two-state solution to achieve lasting peace with an independent Palestinian state.#HakeemJeffries #DonaldTrump #Iran #MiddleEast #Israel #Palestine #USPolitics #Congress #BreakingNews #IranWar #IsraelPalestine #TwoStateSolution #NationalSecurity #GasPrices #WorldNews

articles.title: SC Justice Coney Barrett Jolts Trump Camp; MAGA Erupts Over Supreme Court Election Decision | WATCH
articles.description: A U.S. Supreme Court ruling on mail-in ballots has triggered strong reactions from several Republican lawmakers and conservative commentators. Justice Amy Coney Barrett, nominated by President Donald Trump during his first term, joined Chief Justice John Roberts and the court's liberal justices in upholding state laws that allow certain mail-in ballots postmarked by Election Day to be counted after Election Day. The decision drew criticism from figures including Senator Eric Schmitt and other prominent conservatives, who argued it could affect election integrity. Others noted that late-arriving ballots account for only a small share of total votes and that existing verification procedures remain in place.

articles.title: H-1B Visa Holders Say Texas Is Turning Them Away — Here's Why

articles.title: US Supreme Court rebuffs Donald Trump's appeal in E Jean Carroll case
articles.description: The US Supreme Court declined on Monday to hear President Donald Trump's bid to overturn a $5 million verdict in favor of E Jean Carroll in a case in which a jury found him liable for sexually abusing the former magazine columnist and then defaming her.

articles.title: Quote of the day by American music legend Carole King: 'Get up every morning with a smile on your face and show the world all the love in your heart. Then people are going to treat you better’ - A timeless lesson on spreading kindness, joy and positivity
articles.description: Legendary singer Carole King's quote of the day, 'Get up every morning with a smile on your face and show the world all the love in your heart,' from her hit song 'Beautiful,' offers a powerful reminder. This simple philosophy, rooted in self-love and optimism, encourages a positive outlook that can transform daily interactions and experiences, proving that genuine joy begins from within and can radiate outwards.

articles.title: Draft Uniform Civil Code bill for West Bengal will be produced before state Cabinet on July 2: CM Suvendu Adhikari
articles.description: West Bengal's Chief Minister announced the draft Uniform Civil Code bill will be presented to the state Cabinet on July 2, preceding its Assembly introduction. This move aligns with the BJP's election promise to implement UCC, aiming for a unified set of civil laws across religions. While Goa has a long-standing common code, Uttarakhand, Gujarat, and Assam have recently passed their own UCC legislation.

articles.title: Indian Army to raise 'Baaz Battalions' for drone operations: COAS
articles.description: Army Chief General Upendra Dwivedi announced the raising of specialised 'Baaz Battalions' to strengthen drone-based intelligence, surveillance, and reconnaissance. He said the Army's drone fleet has grown from a few hundred to over 50,000 in two years.

articles.title: Bengaluru: Door-to-door survey for Electoral Roll-2026 from June 30
articles.description: The Greater Bengaluru Authority (GBA) is set to begin a Special Intensive Revision of the Electoral Roll-2026 from June 30. Booth Level Officers will conduct door-to-door visits to distribute enumeration forms, with 2,855 Voter Facilitation Centres set up to assist citizens in the comprehensive updating exercise.

articles.description: Pakistan has suspended Geo News for 15 days over a Muharram broadcast, citing religious sensitivities and risks to public order. The move has renewed concerns over media censorship and sectarian tensions in the country.
